title: Add spring entry/exit animations to B-Tree canvas nodes and edges
description: Live browser testing confirms: B-Tree canvas has ZERO animation between steps. Nodes teleport. Tree redraws instantly. Compare with btree.app which smoothly animates insertions. Add: (1) New node fades in with opacity 0→1 over 200ms ease-out, (2) Promoted key during split slides upward via CSS transform translateY over 300ms, (3) Child nodes spread apart via transform translateX over 200ms, (4) Active node gets a soft glow filter: drop-shadow(0 0 8px rgba(59,130,246,0.4)). Use CSS transitions on SVG group transforms — no additional library needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Inserting a key shows the new node fading in (opacity transition 200ms)
B-Tree split shows the promoted key animating upward to the parent
Child nodes spread apart during split with smooth horizontal movement
Active/highlighted node has a soft blue glow (drop-shadow filter)
All transitions use ease-out or spring timing, NOT linear
Animations are GPU-composited (transform + opacity only, no layout-triggering properties)

title: Add spring animations to Hash Index canvas for insert/resize/overflow
description: Hash Index canvas has zero animation. Insert appears instantly. Resize (doubling buckets) — the most dramatic visual operation — happens in one frame. Add: (1) New entry slides into bucket from top (translateY + opacity, 200ms), (2) Overflow chain extends with new bucket sliding down (300ms), (3) Resize: old buckets fade out, new buckets fade in with stagger (50ms per bucket), entries animate to new positions. (4) Collision: brief shake animation on colliding bucket (CSS keyframe, 200ms).
acceptanceCriteria: Insert: new entry slides into bucket from above with fade-in
Collision: bucket briefly shakes when a collision occurs
Overflow: new overflow bucket slides down below the parent bucket
Resize: old layout fades out, new layout fades in with per-bucket stagger
All animations use transform/opacity only (GPU composited)

title: Add flush and compaction animations to LSM-Tree canvas
description: LSM-Tree flush (memtable→L0 SSTable) and compaction (L0+L1→L1) are the most important visual operations but happen instantly. Add: (1) Flush: memtable box shrinks and transforms into a new L0 SSTable box that slides down to the L0 level (500ms spring), (2) Compaction: source SSTables glow, merge lines draw between them, result SSTable fades in at target level (600ms), (3) Write: new key entry pulses in memtable (100ms green glow).
acceptanceCriteria: Flush: memtable content visually moves down to become a new L0 SSTable (slide + fade)
Compaction: source SSTables highlighted, merge animation, result appears at target level
Write: new entry briefly glows green in memtable
Arrow animations: flow arrows pulse briefly during data movement
All animations respect the step-through pace (don't block user interaction)

title: Replace flat hex fills with subtle gradients and soft glow on all SVG canvases
description: Live screenshots show every SVG node is flat #0f172a with #334155 stroke — monotone and lifeless. Add: (1) SVG linearGradient defs for node fills: from var(--surface) to var(--elevated), (2) Active node filter: drop-shadow(0 0 8px var(--primary)/40) for soft glow, (3) Relationship diamonds: radial gradient from center (primary/10 to transparent), (4) ER entity headers: gradient from var(--elevated) to var(--surface). This replaces hardcoded hex with themed gradients.
acceptanceCriteria: All B-Tree nodes use gradient fill instead of flat color
All Hash buckets use gradient fill instead of flat color
ER entity rectangles and relationship diamonds use gradient fills
Active/highlighted elements have a soft glow (drop-shadow filter)
Gradients use CSS variables for theme compatibility
Visual result: nodes look 3D/polished instead of flat rectangles

title: Add current-step indicator and past-step dimming to Transaction Timeline
description: Live screenshot shows all visible step cards look identical — you can't tell which step you're currently viewing vs which already happened. Add: (1) Current step card: left accent bar (border-l-3 border-primary), slight scale (transform: scale(1.02)), full brightness, (2) Past steps: reduced opacity (0.6), no accent bar, (3) Future steps: not rendered until reached (currently all visible steps are past+current). This creates a clear 'you are here' indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Current step card has a visible left accent bar in primary color
Current step card is slightly scaled up (1.02x) compared to others
Past step cards have reduced opacity (0.6) to recede visually
Clear visual distinction: past (dim) → current (bright + accent) → future (not yet visible)
Scrolls to keep current step visible as user advances

title: Add hover interactions to ER diagram entities and B-Tree nodes
description: Live testing shows zero hover feedback on any SVG element. No glow, no scale, no tooltip. Hovering over an ER entity or B-Tree node gives NO visual response — the user doesn't know it's interactive. Add: (1) ER entities: on hover, subtle scale(1.02) + elevated shadow + cursor:pointer, (2) B-Tree nodes: on hover, border brightens + tooltip showing 'Click to select', (3) Hash buckets: on hover, slight brightness increase. Use CSS :hover on SVG groups or onMouseEnter/Leave handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering ER entity shows subtle scale increase and elevated shadow
Hovering B-Tree node brightens the border color
Hovering Hash bucket shows slight brightness increase
Cursor changes to pointer on all hoverable SVG elements
Hover transitions are smooth (150ms ease-out)

title: Add zoom/pan controls to B-Tree and Hash Index SVG canvases
description: Live testing shows B-Tree and Hash canvases have no zoom or pan. Large trees run off-screen with no way to navigate. Hash tables after resize need horizontal scrolling. Add: (1) Mouse wheel zoom (scale 0.5x to 3x) with smooth transition, (2) Click-drag to pan (when not on a node), (3) Minimap in corner for large visualizations, (4) 'Fit to screen' button that auto-zooms to show entire tree/table. Reference: React Flow (used elsewhere in the app) has these built in.
acceptanceCriteria: Mouse wheel zooms in/out on B-Tree and Hash canvases (0.5x to 3x range)
Click-drag on empty canvas area pans the view
Fit-to-screen button visible in canvas toolbar
Zoom level indicator showing current scale
Smooth zoom transition (not instant jump)

title: Replace key emoji 🔑 with proper SVG icon for primary keys in ER diagram
description: Live screenshot shows 🔑 emoji used as PK indicator in ER entities. Emojis render inconsistently across platforms (different on Mac/Windows/Linux/Android) and look informal. Replace with a small SVG key icon that matches the design system — consistent size, consistent color (gold for PK), crisp at all zoom levels.
acceptanceCriteria: PK indicator uses SVG icon instead of 🔑 emoji
Icon is 12x12px, consistent across all platforms
Icon color matches the PK gold color (#fbbf24 or var(--warning))
FK indicator (if present) uses a different icon (link icon in purple)

title: Create consistent visual design language document for all database visualizations
description: The module has NO visual identity — screenshots look like any generic tool. Create a design spec: (1) Color tokens: primary nodes=var(--primary)/10 fill, active=var(--primary) glow, success=green, warning=amber, error=red, (2) Node shapes: rounded-rect(8px) for all containers, (3) Edge style: 1.5px stroke, rounded joins, arrow markers, (4) Animation timing: entries=200ms ease-out, movements=300ms spring(damping:20), exits=150ms ease-in, (5) Typography: values=monospace 13px, labels=sans 11px, badges=sans 10px bold. This becomes the reference for all future visualization work.
acceptanceCriteria: Design spec document at docs/design/database-visual-language.md
Covers: color tokens, node shapes, edge styles, animation timing, typography
Includes visual examples (can be text descriptions with hex/CSS values)
All future visualization tasks reference this document
At least 5 specific 'before → after' examples showing the style change
